prompt: Create a module called `content_indexer` for a Drupal 10 site. It should use cron to find unindexed content nodes and process them via a queue. Create a QueueWorker plugin that processes each item by marking it as indexed. We want D11 compatibility.
expected_output: A cron+queue module with hook_cron() that adds items to a queue (not processing directly), a QueueWorker plugin with cron.time setting and error handling in processItem(), placed in the correct PSR-4 path
expectations: hook_cron() only discovers work and adds items to queue via \Drupal::queue('content_indexer')->createItem() -- does NOT process heavy work directly in hook_cron()
Queue name in hook_cron createItem matches the QueueWorker plugin ID exactly
QueueWorker plugin annotation/attribute includes cron time setting: cron = {"time" = 30} or cron: {time: 30} or similar (without this, items never auto-process during cron)
processItem() contains try/catch error handling -- distinguishes between retryable errors (RequeueException) and fatal errors
Does NOT use \Drupal::queue() static call inside processItem() -- the queue worker receives items automatically
QueueWorker placed in correct PSR-4 path: src/Plugin/QueueWorker/
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en content_indexer returns exit code 0
E2E: Cron runs without error -- ddev drush cron returns exit code 0
